rationale: Both land on the correct, thread-matching conclusion (real regression, fixed by #4792, already absorbed into the vLLM 0.24 rebase, close with reopen condition) and both cite the exact code diff quoted in the ground truth. X is more tightly grounded — its extra citations (gpu_ar_model_runner.py, _FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES, timeout messages) align with the 'See gpu_ar_model_runner' cross-reference implied by the ground-truth comment, and it engages with reporter-specific environment details (NaN-clamp patch, timeout value differences) the other candidate ignores. Y is solid but pads unverified claims (NPU runner files never mentioned in the thread) and leaks internal pipeline scaffolding ('Run report', task dict) into what should be a clean maintainer-facing answer, hurting completeness/polish.
